problem: Why do amyloid plaques form in Alzheimer's and can they be cleared?
hypothesis: Why do amyloid plaques form in Alzheimer's and can they be cleared? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Monoclonal. Monoclonal antibody blocking protein-protein interaction directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Retrospective cohort analysis with 26 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities. In the expert's mind there are few. — Shunryu Suzuki
